definition: The range between a significant swing high and swing low that price is currently operating within.
feeds_into: premium_discount (dealing range is divided into premium/discount at 50%)
CRT_candle_range_theory (each candle IS a micro dealing range)
related_to: market_structure (structure creates the swings that bound the range)
PO3 (daily dealing range follows the PO3 pattern)
bridge_integration: Dealing range is computed at two scales (M15 trigger and H4 macro) and feeds zone, OTE, and Fibonacci logic. (1) M15 (LTF) range — bridge/ict_pipeline.py:619-630: detect_swings(df_primary, lookback=5) yields swing highs/lows; range_high = max of last 3 swing highs, range_low = min of last 3 swing lows. Fallback if too few swings: max/min over the most recent 50 bars (~12.5h on M15). The choice of 'last 3 swings' is deliberate — wider windows produced OTE zones too wide to score (ARCHITECTURE.md § 5.3). Result lands on SymbolAnalysis.range_high / .range_low. (2) H4 (HTF) range — ict_pipeline.py:641-650: same procedure on df_htf_closed (CLOSED H4 bars only — phantom-FVG bug pattern), populates SymbolAnalysis.htf_range_high / .htf_range_low. (3) Premium/Discount classification — ict_pipeline.py:652-663: get_pd_zone(current_price, range_high, range_low) and pd_aligned_with_bias() set SymbolAnalysis.pd_zone / .pd_aligned (M15) and .htf_pd_zone / .htf_pd_aligned (H4). Both are recomputed at line 1113-1117 after final direction is locked. (4) ZONE GATE (hardest pre-gate) — claude_decision.py:575-585: if pd_zone is set and pd_aligned is False (BUY in premium OR SELL in discount), the trade is SKIPped. ARCHITECTURE.md § 4.1 lists this as gate #6 — '#1 historical loss cause: buying into resistance. Hard block.' (5) HTF ZONE CHECK — claude_decision.py:587-611: a softer M15-vs-H4 conflict check. If htf_pd_zone violates direction, Grade A is capped to B (or SKIP if already C); confluence_factors gets 'HTF_zone_conflict(H4_<zone>)'. (6) OTE zone — ict_pipeline.py:888-898: 0.618-0.786 retracement of (range_high - range_low), used for FVG-in-OTE entry-priority sorting. (7) Fibonacci TP levels — ict_pipeline.py:1046-1047: prefers htf_range_high/low when available, falls back to M15 range. The fib_tp_levels feed claude_decision.py's DOL pre-filter (line 663-671) and TP target prompt. (8) Equilibrium / 50% midpoint — implicit in get_pd_zone (returns 'premium'/'discount'/'mid') and used in synergy_scorer.py:_pd_aligned for the 'FVG in wrong premium/discount zone' (-3) and 'OTE in wrong zone' (-5) gates. (9) Nested dealing ranges (concept claim that H4 contains H1 contains M15) is partially honored: only M15 (trigger) and H4 (macro) are computed in code — H1 is not. The ICT principle 'active range must align with HTF range' is enforced via the HTF Zone Check (#5 above) and the HTF Alignment Gate (claude_decision.py pre-gate #5 — non-scalp direction must agree with H4 bias).

components.range_high: The swing high — also BSL (buy-side liquidity target)
components.range_low: The swing low — also SSL (sell-side liquidity target)
components.equilibrium: 50% of the range — fair value. This is WHERE premium_discount divides.
components.premium: Upper half (above 50%) — this IS the premium_discount concept applied
components.discount: Lower half (below 50%) — this IS the premium_discount concept applied

nested_dealing_ranges: Every timeframe has its own dealing range. H4 range contains H1 ranges, which contain M15 ranges. The ACTIVE dealing range is the one on your trading timeframe, but it must align with the HTF range from market_structure.
connection_note: The dealing range is the BRIDGE between market_structure (which defines the swings) and premium_discount (which classifies where price is). You cannot have premium/discount without a dealing range, and you cannot have a dealing range without structure.
